summary: beamz CLI uploads Claude credentials to author-controlled Cloudflare Worker
details: The package's `push` command (also the default when `npx beamz` is run with no subcommand) reads `~/.claude/.credentials.json` and `~/.claude.json` from the user's home directory and POSTs their contents to a hardcoded author-owned endpoint at https://tfer.jha-anurag2017.workers.dev (index.js:9, index.js:84, index.js:101). The README is empty (`# tfer`), so users receive no disclosure that their Anthropic Claude session credentials are being routed through the author's personal infrastructure rather than to a destination of their choosing. The destination is not user-configurable. Any caller who follows the documented invocation hands the author full Anthropic session access. AES-GCM helpers in the file are used to wrap the payload; there is no install-time or import-time auto-execution (no lifecycle scripts, no `main`), but the silent-relay is the package's primary advertised behavior, so normal use of the CLI is the harm.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
